Powered by Entire:
  This conversation will be linked to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- model-training-eval: Design, debug, and analyze training pipelines for any trainable ML model. Use when Claude needs to architect a training pipeline, diagnose training failures (NaN losses, exploding gradients, plateaus, train-val gaps), audit training dynamics (loss curves, gradient flow, calibration), design evaluation protocols, or optimize training efficiency. Covers LLMs, vision, speech, RL, tabular, multimodal, time-series, and classical ML — framework-agnostic. Trigger whenever the user mentions training a model, fine-tuning, pretraining, SFT, RLHF, debugging a training run, loss curves, gradient issues, hyperparameter tuning, overfitting/underfitting, evaluation metrics, benchmark design, dataset splits, data leakage, mixed precision issues, distributed training failures, or anything about why a model "isn't learning." Use even when the user describes symptoms in casual terms ("my loss is weird", "the model isn't converging", "I think I'm overfitting").
- git-commit: Commit code changes to git with careful review and detailed commit messages. Use when Claude needs to stage and commit code changes to a git repository. This skill ensures changes are reviewed with git diff before staging, excludes markdown files (except README.md), and generates comprehensive commit messages following conventional commit standards.
- code-implementation: Implement code changes with a structured workflow. Use when Claude needs to implement features, fix bugs, or make code changes in a repository. Includes repository exploration, branch creation, implementation planning, unit testing, coverage checks, documentation, and git commits. Ensures quality through testing and proper documentation.
- skill-codex:codex: Use when the user asks to run Codex CLI (codex exec, codex resume) or references OpenAI Codex for code analysis, refactoring, or automated editing
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

input.args: Design and implement simple chatbot UI to test LLM with OpenAI API through bifrost gateway. Mimic how prompt is setup in benchmark pipeline.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/vscode/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.1.0/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec self-review** — quick inline check for placeholders, contradictions, ambiguity, scope (see below)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" [shape=box];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)";
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" -> "User reviews spec?";
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Self-Review:**
After writing the spec document, look at it with fresh eyes:

1. **Placeholder scan:** Any "TBD", "TODO", incomplete sections, or vague requirements? Fix them.
2. **Internal consistency:** Do any sections contradict each other? Does the architecture match the feature descriptions?
3. **Scope check:** Is this focused enough for a single implementation plan, or does it need decomposition?
4. **Ambiguity check:** Could any requirement be interpreted two different ways? If so, pick one and make it explicit.

Fix any issues inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on.

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

content.content:
1	"""Combined workflow quality benchmark for Experiment A.
2	
3	Composes state machine, tool-calling, and chain propagation metrics
4	into a single weighted workflow quality score.
5	
6	CLI usage (invoked by scripts/run_exp_a.sh):
7	    python -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark \\
8	        --model  Qwen/Qwen3-32B \\
9	        --output results/exp_a/Qwen_Qwen3-32B_auto.json \\
10	        --data   data/output/benchmark/task_a/ \\
11	        --endpoint http://localhost:8000
12	
13	Data directory is expected to contain JSONL files produced by
14	scripts/generate_benchmark_data.sh (one file per complexity level).
15	Each sample must have the schema written by generate_workflows.py.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from typing import Any
22	
23	import structlog
24	
25	from llm_workflow_agents.eval.state_accuracy import StateMachineMetrics, parse_state_transitions
26	from llm_workflow_agents.eval.tool_call_f1 import ToolCallMetrics, parse_tool_calls
27	from llm_workflow_agents.eval.tool_chain_propagation import ChainPropagationMetrics
28	from llm_workflow_agents.data.system_prompt import build_enriched_system_prompt as _build_system_prompt
29	
30	logger = structlog.get_logger(__name__)
31	
32	
33	@dataclass
34	class WorkflowQualityMetrics:
35	    """Combined workflow quality metrics."""
36	
37	    full_workflow_success: float = 0.0  # Target: >=55%
38	    weighted_workflow_score: float = 0.0  # Target: >=0.75
39	    latency_per_turn_median_ms: float = 0.0  # Target: <=2000 (L1-L3), <=5000 (L4-L5)
40	    latency_per_turn_avg_ms: float = 0.0
41	    ttft_avg_ms: float = 0.0
42	
43	    state_metrics: StateMachineMetrics = field(default_factory=StateMachineMetrics)
44	    tool_metrics: ToolCallMetrics = field(default_factory=ToolCallMetrics)
45	    tool_metrics_conversation: ToolCallMetrics = field(default_factory=ToolCallMetrics)
46	    chain_metrics: ChainPropagationMetrics = field(default_factory=ChainPropagationMetrics)
47	
48	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
49	        return {
50	            "full_workflow_success": self.full_workflow_success,
51	            "weighted_workflow_score": self.weighted_workflow_score,
52	            "latency_per_turn_median_ms": self.latency_per_turn_median_ms,
53	            "latency_per_turn_avg_ms": self.latency_per_turn_avg_ms,
54	            "ttft_avg_ms": self.ttft_avg_ms,
55	            "state_metrics": self.state_metrics.to_dict(),
56	            "tool_metrics": self.tool_metrics.to_dict(),
57	            "tool_metrics_conversation": self.tool_metrics_conversation.to_dict(),
58	            "chain_metrics": self.chain_metrics.to_dict(),
59	        }
60	
61	
62	def compute_weighted_score(
63	    state: StateMachineMetrics,
64	    tool: ToolCallMetrics,
65	    completion: float,
66	) -> float:
67	    """Compute weighted workflow quality score.
68	
69	    Uses the *better* of per-turn and conversation-level state accuracy
70	    (``state_sequence_accuracy``) so that models which traverse the
71	    correct states in fewer turns are not unfairly penalised.
72	
73	    Formula: 0.4 * max(state_turn, state_seq) + 0.4 * ToolCallF1 + 0.2 * TaskCompletion
74	
75	    Args:
76	        state: State machine metrics.
77	        tool: Tool-calling metrics.
78	        completion: Task completion rate (0.0-1.0).
79	
80	    Returns:
81	        Weighted score between 0.0 and 1.0.
82	    """
83	    best_state_acc = max(
84	        state.state_transition_accuracy,
85	        state.state_sequence_accuracy,
86	    )
87	    return (
88	        0.4 * best_state_acc
89	        + 0.4 * tool.tool_call_f1
90	        + 0.2 * completion
91	    )
92	
93	
94	def compute_full_workflow_success(
95	    state: StateMachineMetrics,
96	    tool: ToolCallMetrics,
97	    chain: ChainPropagationMetrics,
98	) -> float:
99	    """Compute full workflow success rate.
100	
101	    A workflow is fully successful if:
102	      - Task was completed (reached terminal state)
103	      - All tool calls were correct (F1 >= 0.8)
104	      - Chain propagation was correct (accuracy >= 0.7)
105	
106	    Returns approximate rate based on component metrics.
107	    """
108	    # Estimate: multiply independent success probabilities
109	    completion_factor = state.task_completion_rate
110	    tool_factor = min(tool.tool_call_f1 / 0.8, 1.0) if tool.tool_call_f1 > 0 else 0.0
111	    chain_factor = (
112	        min(chain.chain_propagation_accuracy / 0.7, 1.0)
113	        if chain.total_chains > 0
114	        else 1.0  # No chains present = not a failure
115	    )
116	
117	    return completion_factor * tool_factor * chain_factor
118	
119	
120	def compute_latency_median(latencies_ms: list[float]) -> float:
121	    """Compute median latency from a list of per-turn latencies."""
122	    if not latencies_ms:
123	        return 0.0
124	    sorted_lat = sorted(latencies_ms)
125	    n = len(sorted_lat)
126	    if n % 2 == 0:
127	        return (sorted_lat[n // 2 - 1] + sorted_lat[n // 2]) / 2
128	    return sorted_lat[n // 2]
129	
130	
131	def compute_average(values: list[float]) -> float:
132	    """Compute arithmetic mean from a list of values."""
133	    if not values:
134	        return 0.0
135	    return sum(values) / len(values)
136

138	def evaluate_workflow_quality(
139	    state_metrics: StateMachineMetrics,
140	    tool_metrics: ToolCallMetrics,
141	    chain_metrics: ChainPropagationMetrics,
142	    latencies_ms: list[float] | None = None,
143	    ttfts_ms: list[float] | None = None,
144	    tool_metrics_turn: ToolCallMetrics | None = None,
145	    tool_metrics_conversation: ToolCallMetrics | None = None,
146	) -> WorkflowQualityMetrics:
147	    """Compute combined workflow quality metrics.
148	
149	    Args:
150	        state_metrics: State machine adherence results.
151	        tool_metrics: Tool-calling metrics used for composite scoring
152	            (typically the better of per-turn and conversation-level).
153	        chain_metrics: Tool chain propagation results.
154	        latencies_ms: Optional per-turn latency measurements.
155	        ttfts_ms: Optional per-turn TTFT measurements.
156	        tool_metrics_turn: Per-turn tool metrics (for reporting).
157	        tool_metrics_conversation: Conversation-level tool metrics (for reporting).
158	
159	    Returns:
160	        WorkflowQualityMetrics with combined scores.
161	    """
162	    weighted = compute_weighted_score(
163	        state_metrics,
164	        tool_metrics,
165	        state_metrics.task_completion_rate,
166	    )
167	
168	    full_success = compute_full_workflow_success(
169	        state_metrics,
170	        tool_metrics,
171	        chain_metrics,
172	    )
173	
174	    latencies = latencies_ms or []
175	    ttfts = ttfts_ms or []
176	    median_latency = compute_latency_median(latencies)
177	    avg_latency = compute_average(latencies)
178	    avg_ttft = compute_average(ttfts)
179	
180	    metrics = WorkflowQualityMetrics(
181	        full_workflow_success=full_success,
182	        weighted_workflow_score=weighted,
183	        latency_per_turn_median_ms=median_latency,
184	        latency_per_turn_avg_ms=avg_latency,
185	        ttft_avg_ms=avg_ttft,
186	        state_metrics=state_metrics,
187	        tool_metrics=tool_metrics,
188	        tool_metrics_conversation=tool_metrics_conversation or tool_metrics,
189	        chain_metrics=chain_metrics,
190	    )
191	
192	    logger.info(
193	        "workflow_quality_eval_complete",
194	        weighted_score=weighted,
195	        full_success=full_success,
196	        median_latency_ms=median_latency,
197	        avg_latency_ms=avg_latency,
198	        avg_ttft_ms=avg_ttft,
199	    )
200	
201	    return metrics
202

208	def _load_samples(data_path: "Path") -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
209	    """Load benchmark samples from a directory (all *.jsonl) or a single file."""
210	    import json
211	    from pathlib import Path
212	
213	    p = Path(data_path)
214	    if p.is_file():
215	        paths = [p]
216	    else:
217	        paths = sorted(p.glob("*.jsonl"))
218	
219	    samples: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
220	    for path in paths:
221	        with open(path) as f:
222	            for line in f:
223	                line = line.strip()
224	                if line:
225	                    samples.append(json.loads(line))
226	    return samples

229	def _downgrade_tool_turns_to_text(messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
230	    """Convert past structured tool turns to plain text.
231	
232	    Gemini-3 (via BiFrost) requires a server-generated ``thought_signature`` on
233	    every re-sent ``functionCall`` part. BiFrost's OpenAI translation strips
234	    that field, so any conversation containing a past ``assistant.tool_calls``
235	    fails the next request with HTTP 400. We sidestep the validation by
236	    rewriting past tool turns:
237	
238	      - assistant + tool_calls -> assistant with ``<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>``
239	        tags appended in ``content``.
240	      - role=tool -> role=user with ``[Tool result] ...`` prefix.
241	
242	    The ``tools=[...]`` schema in the request is unchanged, so the model can
243	    still emit *new* structured tool calls; only past ones are textualised.
244	    """
245	    import json as _json
246	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
247	    for msg in messages:
248	        role = msg.get("role")
249	        if role == "assistant" and msg.get("tool_calls"):
250	            tags: list[str] = []
251	            for tc in msg.get("tool_calls", []):
252	                fn = tc.get("function", {})
253	                args = fn.get("arguments", {})
254	                if isinstance(args, str):
255	                    try:
256	                        args = _json.loads(args)
257	                    except _json.JSONDecodeError:
258	                        pass
259	                tags.append(_json.dumps({"name": fn.get("name", ""), "arguments": args}))
260	            content = msg.get("content") or ""
261	            tag_block = "\n".join(f"<tool_call>{t}</tool_call>" for t in tags)
262	            new_content = (content + ("\n" if content else "") + tag_block).strip()
263	            out.append({"role": "assistant", "content": new_content})
264	        elif role == "tool":
265	            tcid = msg.get("tool_call_id", "")
266	            prefix = f"[Tool result {tcid}]" if tcid else "[Tool result]"
267	            out.append({"role": "user", "content": f"{prefix}: {msg.get('content', '')}"})
268	        else:
269	            out.append(msg)
270	    return out
271	
272	
273	def _call_vllm(
274	    endpoint: str,
275	    model: str,
276	    messages: list[dict[str, Any]],
277	    temperature: float = 0.0,
278	    max_tokens: int = 1024,
279	    tools: list[dict[str, Any]] | None = None,
280	    enable_thinking: bool = False,
281	    engine: str = "vllm",
282	) -> tuple[str, list[dict[str, Any]], float, float]:
283	    """Call the OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint (vLLM, SGLang, TRT-LLM, or BiFrost).
284	
285	    When *tools* are provided they are included in the request so that
286	    the server can emit structured tool calls.
287	
288	    Uses streaming to measure TTFT (Time To First Token).
289	
290	    Returns:
291	        (content, raw_tool_calls, latency_ms, ttft_ms)
292	
293	    *content* has any structured tool calls serialised as
294	    ``<tool_call>{JSON}</tool_call>`` tags appended so that
295	    ``tool_call_f1.parse_tool_calls`` can extract them.
296	
297	    *raw_tool_calls* is the list straight from the API response so the
298	    caller can build a well-formed assistant message for the context
299	    (with ``tool_calls`` field and matching ``tool_call_id``).
300	    """
301	    import json
302	    import time
303	    import urllib.error
304	    import urllib.request
305	
306	    if engine == "bifrost":
307	        messages = _downgrade_tool_turns_to_text(messages)
308	
309	    request_body: dict[str, Any] = {
310	        "model": model,
311	        "messages": messages,
312	        "temperature": temperature,
313	        "max_tokens": max_tokens,
314	        "stream": True,
315	    }
316	    if engine in {"vllm", "sglang"}:
317	        # Qwen3-family thinking toggle. Ignored by tokenizer chat templates
318	        # that don't reference enable_thinking (Gemma, Mistral, etc.), so it's
319	        # safe to always send for vLLM and SGLang (≥0.5 accepts the field).
320	        # TRT-LLM rejects unknown body fields, so it stays in the else branch.
321	        # Default False for fair latency comparison against non-thinking models.
322	        request_body["chat_template_kwargs"] = {"enable_thinking": enable_thinking}
323	    if tools:
324	        request_body["tools"] = tools
325	    payload = json.dumps(request_body).encode()
326	
327	    req = urllib.request.Request(
328	        f"{endpoint.rstrip('/')}/v1/chat/completions",
329	        data=payload,
330	        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
331	        method="POST",
332	    )
333	
334	    t0 = time.monotonic()
335	    ttft_ms = 0.0
336	    first_token_received = False
337	    content_parts: list[str] = []
338	    raw_tool_calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
339	    # Track tool call deltas keyed by index
340	    tool_call_accum: dict[int, dict[str, Any]] = {}
341	
342	    # Generous default because hybrid MoE models on enforce_eager (Qwen3.6
343	    # with TurboQuant, Gemma-4) may JIT kernels during early inference,
344	    # stretching any single socket read past 120 s. Override via env var if
345	    # needed for even slower cold starts.
346	    import os
347	
348	    read_timeout_s = float(os.environ.get("VLLM_HTTP_READ_TIMEOUT_S", "600"))
349	    try:
350	        resp_cm = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=read_timeout_s)
351	    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
352	        # 400-class errors usually mean a malformed conversation slipped through
353	        # (e.g. mismatched tool_call_ids, schema-incompatible arguments). Log
354	        # the response body once and return an empty turn so the benchmark
355	        # continues instead of aborting all 200 samples.
356	        try:
357	            body = e.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")[:1000]
358	        except Exception:
359	            body = "<no body>"
360	        logger.warning(
361	            "http_error_during_call",
362	            status=e.code,
363	            body=body,
364	            messages_len=len(messages),
365	            last_role=messages[-1].get("role") if messages else None,
366	        )
367	        latency_ms = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000.0
368	        return "", [], latency_ms, 0.0
369	    with resp_cm as resp:
370	        for raw_line in resp:
371	            line = raw_line.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").strip()
372	            if not line or not line.startswith("data:"):
373	                continue
374	            data_str = line[len("data:"):].strip()
375	            if data_str == "[DONE]":
376	                break
377	            try:
378	                chunk = json.loads(data_str)
379	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
380	                continue
381	            delta = chunk.get("choices", [{}])[0].get("delta", {})
382	
383	            # Measure TTFT on first content or tool_call delta
384	            if not first_token_received and (delta.get("content") or delta.get("tool_calls")):
385	                ttft_ms = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000.0
386	                first_token_received = True
387	
388	            if delta.get("content"):
389	                content_parts.append(delta["content"])
390	
391	            # Accumulate streamed tool call deltas
392	            for tc_delta in delta.get("tool_calls", []):
393	                tc_idx = tc_delta.get("index", 0)
394	                if tc_idx not in tool_call_accum:
395	                    tool_call_accum[tc_idx] = {
396	                        "id": tc_delta.get("id", ""),
397	                        "type": "function",
398	                        "function": {"name": "", "arguments": ""},
399	                    }
400	                entry = tool_call_accum[tc_idx]
401	                if tc_delta.get("id"):
402	                    entry["id"] = tc_delta["id"]
403	                fn_delta = tc_delta.get("function", {})
404	                if fn_delta.get("name"):
405	                    entry["function"]["name"] += fn_delta["name"]
406	                if fn_delta.get("arguments"):
407	                    entry["function"]["arguments"] += fn_delta["arguments"]
408	
409	    latency_ms = (time.monotonic() - t0) * 1000.0
410	
411	    # Build raw_tool_calls from accumulated deltas
412	    for idx in sorted(tool_call_accum):
413	        raw_tool_calls.append(tool_call_accum[idx])
414	
415	    content = "".join(content_parts)
416	
417	    # Append any structured tool calls as <tool_call> tags so that
418	    # parse_tool_calls() can extract them.
419	    for tc in raw_tool_calls:
420	        fn = tc.get("function", {})
421	        call_obj = {"name": fn.get("name", ""), "arguments": fn.get("arguments", {})}
422	        # arguments may arrive as a JSON string — parse it
423	        if isinstance(call_obj["arguments"], str):
424	            try:
425	                call_obj["arguments"] = json.loads(call_obj["arguments"])
426	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
427	                pass
428	        content += f"\n<tool_call>{json.dumps(call_obj)}</tool_call>"
429	
430	    return content, raw_tool_calls, latency_ms, ttft_ms
431

433	def _replay_conversation(
434	    endpoint: str,
435	    model: str,
436	    sample: dict[str, Any],
437	    temperature: float = 0.0,
438	    enable_thinking: bool = False,
439	    engine: str = "vllm",
440	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[float], list[float]]:
441	    """Replay a conversation, substituting model completions at assistant turns.
442	
443	    Ground-truth tool responses are kept as-is so the conversation stays on
444	    track regardless of whether the model's tool call was correct — this
445	    isolates state-transition and tool-call quality from cascading failures.
446	
447	    Returns:
448	        (predicted_messages, latencies_ms_per_assistant_turn, ttfts_ms_per_assistant_turn)
449	    """
450	    tools = sample.get("tool_schemas") or []
451	    terminal_states = set(sample.get("workflow_graph", {}).get("terminal", []))
452	    predicted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
453	    latencies_ms: list[float] = []
454	    ttfts_ms: list[float] = []
455	    context: list[dict[str, Any]] = []  # sliding context sent to the model
456	    pending_tool_call_ids: list[str] = []  # ids from the latest assistant tool_calls
457	
458	    for msg in sample.get("messages", []):
459	        role = msg["role"]
460	
461	        if role == "system":
462	            enriched = _build_system_prompt(sample, msg["content"])
463	            context.append({"role": "system", "content": enriched})
464	            predicted.append(msg)  # keep original in predictions for eval
465	
466	        elif role == "user":
467	            context.append({"role": "user", "content": msg["content"]})
468	            predicted.append(msg)
469	
470	        elif role == "assistant":
471	            # Some samples open with a system-initiated assistant greeting
472	            # before any user turn (system → assistant → user → ...). Models
473	            # can't be asked to predict a preamble issued before any user
474	            # input, and Qwen3-family chat templates explicitly reject a
475	            # message list with no user query. Use the ground-truth turn
476	            # as a fixed preamble: append to context and predictions, no
477	            # model call, no recorded latency.
478	            if not any(c.get("role") == "user" for c in context):
479	                logger.info(
480	                    "skip_preamble_assistant_turn",
481	                    reason="no_user_in_context",
482	                    turn=len(predicted),
483	                )
484	                preamble: dict[str, Any] = {
485	                    "role": "assistant",
486	                    "content": msg.get("content", ""),
487	                }
488	                if msg.get("tool_calls"):
489	                    preamble["tool_calls"] = msg["tool_calls"]
490	                    pending_tool_call_ids.clear()
491	                    for tc in msg["tool_calls"]:
492	                        tc_id = tc.get("id", "")
493	                        if tc_id:
494	                            pending_tool_call_ids.append(tc_id)
495	                context.append(preamble)
496	                predicted.append(msg)
497	                continue
498	
499	            content, raw_tool_calls, latency, ttft = _call_vllm(
500	                endpoint, model, context, temperature, tools=tools,
501	                enable_thinking=enable_thinking, engine=engine,
502	            )
503	            latencies_ms.append(latency)
504	            ttfts_ms.append(ttft)
505	            logger.debug(
506	                "model_response",
507	                turn=len(predicted),
508	                latency_ms=round(latency, 1),
509	                ttft_ms=round(ttft, 1),
510	                content=content[:2000],  # truncate to avoid flooding logs
511	            )
512	
513	            # Fix #2: synthesize structured tool_calls from inline <tool_call>
514	            # text tags when the API didn't return them in the structured field.
515	            # Without this the next tool-role message has no tool_call_id and
516	            # frontier providers reject the conversation with HTTP 400.
517	            synthesized_from_text = False
518	            if not raw_tool_calls:
519	                parsed = parse_tool_calls(content)
520	                if parsed:
521	                    import json as _json
522	                    import uuid as _uuid
523	                    raw_tool_calls = [
524	                        {
525	                            "id": f"call_{_uuid.uuid4().hex[:24]}",
526	                            "type": "function",
527	                            "function": {
528	                                "name": p.get("name", ""),
529	                                "arguments": _json.dumps(p.get("arguments", {})),
530	                            },
531	                        }
532	                        for p in parsed
533	                    ]
534	                    synthesized_from_text = True
535	
536	            # Build a well-formed assistant message for the context so
537	            # that subsequent tool-role messages have matching tool_call_ids.
538	            # Use content *without* appended <tool_call> tags for the API context
539	            if raw_tool_calls and not synthesized_from_text:
540	                text_content = content.split("\n<tool_call>")[0]
541	            elif synthesized_from_text:
542	                # Strip ALL <tool_call>...</tool_call> blocks from content for context
543	                import re as _re
544	                text_content = _re.sub(
545	                    r"\s*<tool_call>.*?</tool_call>\s*",
546	                    " ",
547	                    content,
548	                    flags=_re.DOTALL,
549	                ).strip()
550	            else:
551	                text_content = content
552	            ctx_msg: dict[str, Any] = {"role": "assistant", "content": text_content}
553	            if raw_tool_calls:
554	                ctx_msg["tool_calls"] = raw_tool_calls
555	                # Store the ids so the next tool message(s) can reference them
556	                pending_tool_call_ids.clear()
557	                for tc in raw_tool_calls:
558	                    tc_id = tc.get("id", "")
559	                    if tc_id:
560	                        pending_tool_call_ids.append(tc_id)
561	            context.append(ctx_msg)
562	
563	            # For eval, store the full content with <tool_call> tags
564	            pred_msg: dict[str, Any] = {"role": "assistant", "content": content}
565	            predicted.append(pred_msg)
566	
567	            # Fix #1: do NOT early-exit on terminal state. Multi-turn
568	            # negotiations (L1_002, L1_004) reach TERMINAL on turn 1 if the
569	            # model collapses negotiation; truncating the loop loses every
570	            # subsequent GT tool call from per-turn alignment. Walk every
571	            # GT turn instead — the natural end of `for msg in messages`
572	            # provides the stop condition.
573	            if terminal_states:
574	                transitions = parse_state_transitions([pred_msg])
575	                if transitions and transitions[-1][1] in terminal_states:
576	                    logger.info(
577	                        "terminal_state_reached_continuing",
578	                        state=transitions[-1][1],
579	                        turn=len(latencies_ms),
580	                    )
581	
582	        elif role == "tool":
583	            # Use ground-truth tool response to avoid cascading failures.
584	            # Assign tool_call_id from the model's preceding tool call so
585	            # the OpenAI-format conversation stays well-formed.
586	            tool_msg: dict[str, Any] = {
587	                "role": "tool",
588	                "content": msg["content"],
589	            }
590	            if pending_tool_call_ids:
591	                tool_msg["tool_call_id"] = pending_tool_call_ids.pop(0)
592	            elif msg.get("tool_call_id"):
593	                tool_msg["tool_call_id"] = msg["tool_call_id"]
594	            context.append(tool_msg)
595	            predicted.append(msg)
596	
597	    return predicted, latencies_ms, ttfts_ms
598

664	            "Default OFF for fair latency comparison against non-thinking models. "
665	            "Sends chat_template_kwargs={enable_thinking: True} on every request; "
666	            "ignored by tokenizer templates that don't reference the flag."

679	            "'tensorrt_llm' skips the thinking toggle (TRT-LLM rejects "

711	    _start_level_match = _re_start.match(r"(l[1-5])_", data_dir.name) if data_dir.is_file() else None
712	    _start_level_tag = _start_level_match.group(1).upper() if _start_level_match else "mixed"
713	
714	    logger.info(
715	        "benchmark_start",
716	        model=args.model,
717	        engine=args.engine,
718	        endpoint=args.endpoint,
719	        data_dir=str(data_dir),
720	        complexity_level=_start_level_tag,
721	        num_samples=len(samples),
722	        stochastic_trials=args.stochastic_trials,
723	    )
724	
725	    # --- Run deterministic evaluation pass (temperature=0.0) ---
726	    state_predictions: list[ConversationPrediction] = []
727	    state_ground_truths: list[ConversationGroundTruth] = []
728	    tool_predictions: list[TurnPrediction] = []
729	    tool_ground_truths: list[TurnGroundTruth] = []
730	    # Per-conversation tool call lists for conversation-level eval
731	    conv_tool_preds: list[list[TurnPrediction]] = []
732	    conv_tool_gts: list[list[TurnGroundTruth]] = []
733	    chain_predictions: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
734	    chain_ground_truths: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
735	    all_latencies_ms: list[float] = []
736	    all_ttfts_ms: list[float] = []
737	
738	    for idx, sample in enumerate(samples):
739	        conv_id = sample.get("conversation_id", f"sample_{idx}")
740	        tool_schemas = sample.get("tool_schemas", [])
741	        gt_truth = sample.get("ground_truth", {})
742	        terminal_states = [gt_truth.get("terminal_state", "")] if gt_truth.get("terminal_state") else []
743	
744	        logger.info("evaluating_sample", idx=idx + 1, total=len(samples), conversation_id=conv_id)
745	
746	        pred_messages, latencies, ttfts = _replay_conversation(
747	            args.endpoint, args.model, sample, temperature=0.0,
748	            enable_thinking=args.enable_thinking, engine=args.engine,
749	        )
750	        all_latencies_ms.extend(latencies)
751	        all_ttfts_ms.extend(ttfts)
752	
753	        # State machine inputs
754	        state_predictions.append(ConversationPrediction(
755	            conversation_id=conv_id,
756	            messages=pred_messages,
757	        ))
758	        state_ground_truths.append(ConversationGroundTruth(
759	            conversation_id=conv_id,
760	            messages=sample.get("messages", []),
761	            terminal_states=terminal_states,
762	        ))
763	
764	        # Tool-call inputs — one TurnPrediction/GroundTruth per assistant turn
765	        this_conv_preds: list[TurnPrediction] = []
766	        this_conv_gts: list[TurnGroundTruth] = []
767	        for turn_idx, (pred_msg, gt_msg) in enumerate(
768	            zip(pred_messages, sample.get("messages", []))
769	        ):
770	            if gt_msg.get("role") != "assistant":
771	                continue
772	            tp = TurnPrediction(
773	                turn_id=turn_idx,
774	                content=pred_msg.get("content", ""),
775	            )
776	            gt_tool_calls = (gt_msg.get("annotations") or {}).get("tool_calls") or []
777	            tg = TurnGroundTruth(
778	                turn_id=turn_idx,
779	                tool_calls=gt_tool_calls,
780	            )
781	            tool_predictions.append(tp)
782	            tool_ground_truths.append(tg)
783	            this_conv_preds.append(tp)
784	            this_conv_gts.append(tg)
785	        conv_tool_preds.append(this_conv_preds)
786	        conv_tool_gts.append(this_conv_gts)
787	
788	        # Chain propagation inputs
789	        chain_predictions.append({"messages": pred_messages})
790	        chain_ground_truths.append({"messages": sample.get("messages", [])})
791	
792	    # --- Stochastic trials for pass^k ---
793	    stochastic_map: dict[str, list[list[dict[str, Any]]]] = {
794	        s.conversation_id: [] for s in state_predictions
795	    }
796	    for trial_num in range(args.stochastic_trials):
797	        logger.info("stochastic_trial", trial=trial_num + 1, total=args.stochastic_trials)
798	        for idx, sample in enumerate(samples):
799	            conv_id = sample.get("conversation_id", f"sample_{idx}")
800	            trial_messages, _, _ = _replay_conversation(
801	                args.endpoint, args.model, sample, temperature=0.7,
802	                enable_thinking=args.enable_thinking, engine=args.engine,
803	            )
804	            stochastic_map[conv_id].append(trial_messages)
805	
806	    for pred in state_predictions:
807	        pred.stochastic_trials = stochastic_map.get(pred.conversation_id, [])
808	
809	    # --- Compute metrics ---
810	    state_metrics = evaluate_state_machine(state_predictions, state_ground_truths)
811	    tool_metrics_turn = evaluate_tool_calls(tool_predictions, tool_ground_truths)
812	    tool_metrics_conv = evaluate_tool_calls_conversation(conv_tool_preds, conv_tool_gts)
813	    chain_metrics = evaluate_chain_propagation(chain_predictions, chain_ground_truths)
814	
815	    # Use the better of per-turn and conversation-level tool metrics for the
816	    # composite score.  Conversation-level is more lenient — a correct tool
817	    # call at a different turn still gets credit — which is fairer for
818	    # pre-trained models that haven't been fine-tuned on the exact workflow.
819	    tool_metrics_best = (
820	        tool_metrics_conv if tool_metrics_conv.tool_call_f1 >= tool_metrics_turn.tool_call_f1
821	        else tool_metrics_turn
822	    )
823	    quality = evaluate_workflow_quality(
824	        state_metrics, tool_metrics_best, chain_metrics, all_latencies_ms,
825	        ttfts_ms=all_ttfts_ms,
826	        tool_metrics_turn=tool_metrics_turn,
827	        tool_metrics_conversation=tool_metrics_conv,
828	    )
829	
830	    # --- Write results ---
831	    output_path = Path(args.output)
832	    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
833	
834	    # Derive complexity level from data path when --data is a single
835	    # `l[1-5]_*.jsonl` file (the per-level case). For directory inputs
836	    # spanning all levels, "mixed" is recorded.
837	    import re as _re
838	    level_match = _re.match(r"(l[1-5])_", data_dir.name) if data_dir.is_file() else None
839	    level_tag = level_match.group(1).upper() if level_match else "mixed"
840	
841	    result = {
842	        "model": args.model,
843	        "engine": args.engine,
844	        "endpoint": args.endpoint,
845	        "kv_cache_dtype": args.kv_cache_dtype,
846	        "data_dir": str(data_dir),
847	        "complexity_level": level_tag,
848	        "num_samples": len(samples),
849	        "stochastic_trials": args.stochastic_trials,
850	        "metrics": quality.to_dict(),
851	    }
852	
853	    with open(output_path, "w") as f:
854	        json.dump(result, f, indent=2)
855	
856	    logger.info("benchmark_complete", output=str(output_path), **quality.to_dict())
857	    print(f"\nResults written to {output_path}")
858	    print(f"  weighted_workflow_score : {quality.weighted_workflow_score:.3f}  (target >=0.75)")
859	    print(f"  full_workflow_success   : {quality.full_workflow_success:.3f}  (target >=0.55)")
860	    print(f"  state_trans_acc (turn)   : {quality.state_metrics.state_transition_accuracy:.3f}  (target >=0.85)")
861	    print(f"  state_seq_acc (conv)    : {quality.state_metrics.state_sequence_accuracy:.3f}")
862	    print(f"  tool_call_f1 (turn)     : {quality.tool_metrics.tool_call_f1:.3f}  (target >=0.85)")
863	    print(f"  tool_call_f1 (conv)     : {quality.tool_metrics_conversation.tool_call_f1:.3f}")
864	    print(f"  latency_per_turn_avg_ms : {quality.latency_per_turn_avg_ms:.1f}")
865	    print(f"  ttft_avg_ms             : {quality.ttft_avg_ms:.1f}")
